texto: We choose our truths the way we choose our gods, single-sightedly, single-mindedly, no other way to feel or see or think. We lock ourselves into our ways, and click all the truths to one.We put our truths together in pieces, but you use nails and I use glue. You mend with staples. I mend with screws. You stitch what I would bandage.Your truth may not look like mine, but that is not what matters. What matters is this: You can look at a scar and see hurt, or you can look at a scar and see healing. Try to understand.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sheri Reynolds: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “We choose our truths the way we choose our gods, single-sightedly, single-mindedly, no other way …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, healing, truths pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.

frase_original: We choose our truths the way we choose our gods, single-sightedly, single-mindedly, no other way to feel or see or think. We lock ourselves into our ways, and click all the truths to one.We put our truths together in pieces, but you use nails and I use glue. You mend with staples. I mend with screws. You stitch what I would bandage.Your truth may not look like mine, but that is not what matters. What matters is this: You can look at a scar and see hurt, or you can look at a scar and see healing. Try to understand.

texto: Energy is neither created nor destroyed. It just changes shape.
explicacao: Citação de Sheri Reynolds sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Energy is neither created nor destroyed. It just changes shape.” articula reflexao, cemetary, death em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e mudanca.
